item: Traditional cultural practices should be preserved even when they conflict with modern values.

flip_turn.quote: Saya setuju dengan pendapat Anda tentang pentingnya menjaga tradisi yang sesuai dengan hak asasi manusia dan etika modern.

notes: Both agents open from similarly hedged ground — Agent A at P=0.499 (neutral/hedged, not a strong pro-tradition stance) and Agent B at P=0.332 (skeptical-conditional). Neither agent defends the statement's strong claim ('even when conflicting with modern values'), so the debate lacks genuine initial opposition on that axis. Agent A's turn 3 immediately concedes to B's conditional-preservation framing ('I agree with your opinion'), then partially recovers in turn 5 by defending Indonesian traditions as actually compatible with modern values. Agent B holds its core stance firmly throughout (P: 0.332→0.339), while Agent A drifts from neutral toward B's skeptical-conditional position (P: 0.499→0.403). Drift is toward_b but modest. Language-holding PASS: no code-switching or foreign-language bleed detected — the Fix 2 language prohibition appears to have worked. Agent B's turn 4 self-applies the critique to US traditions (segregation, discriminatory laws), showing genuine engagement rather than one-sided attack. Engagement scores are equal (5/5) because both agents consistently reference the other's specific points, but mostly through agreement-with-caveats rather than direct rebuttal. The primary diagnostic concern: this item produced near-zero initial tension (A opens neutral, not pro-tradition), which limits what the debate can demonstrate about drift.
